## [00:00 - 03:01]

Now, this is using Facebook's ads reporting product, which is different to ads manager in that it gives you, I think, far better information. It's far faster to drill down into your breakdowns and it's easier to get insights. Now, this is ads reporting. It's available in the Mega menu. And once you click in, it gives you the opportunity to create a new report. Now, when you create that new report, you build your columns and then you can add your breakdowns here. So you've got the metrics you can choose from. You can see that I've selected a few already and I'll talk about those. And then you can add your breakdowns. So the reason why I like this is, you know, there's a few different reasons. Number one is you can add custom metrics. So in ads manager, one of the frustrations I have, especially when I'm looking at my return on ad spend is I want to look at average order value. I want to look at how well my campaigns, my ad sets and ads are performing with average order value because sometimes I run different promotions on different products. Sometimes there's slightly different funnels. So with this method of adding a custom metric, you can actually create your own data and create more sense from it as well. So this is how you create it. When you get into this section, give it a name, you've got options for the format. You can give it a description and then you add your formula. And what's really cool about this is when you're changing the values, it gives you the options of what you can add in here. So when you type in purchase, it comes up with purchase. So for example, average order value is purchases conversion value, so my revenue, divided by purchases. Now you could use unique purchases, but for this purpose, I'm okay with just using purchases here. And then you can choose which reports it appears in. So if I just hit duplicate here, you can see how this actually works. So for example, if I wanted to put unique purchases, I can find it here. Let's go. So metric is unique purchases. Let's call it AOV. Let's call it UAAV and we'll call it unique average order value and we'll create that metric. So that will give us an extra column. It'll put the column at the end. So let's have a look if that is actually any different. And we'll move it across. Here we go. And so what this is taking into account is total revenue over the unique purchases that have been created. So for example, you can see here, I'm looking at the last 30 days on this particular account
